data.title: Storage: import/export + git-backed mirror + checkpoint strategy

fields.description: Review the storage layer for portability. (1) Export workspace to a git-friendly format (markdown mirror with frontmatter, or JSON) and import with version-gating (SPEC §3 mirror). (2) Consider backing options: SQLite file is fine, but document a checkpoint/backup strategy (WAL checkpoint, copy-on-write). (3) autoexport setting to keep a mirror in sync on every write for git review. This unlocks human review + disaster recovery.

work_log.author: pi
work_log.notes: Implemented cards export (JSONL). Local command, reads SQLite directly. Format: {type:card|event|user, data:{...}} per line. Cards include embedded comments+links. Events are the full audit log. Verified: 48 cards, 243 events, 53 comments, 25 links, 5 users exported from the demo workspace. Import remains TODO.

comments.author: pi
comments.body: PARTIAL PROGRESS — export shipped (JSONL). cards export --workspace <dir> [--out file] dumps all card data (cards+links+comments, events, users) as JSONL, one object per line. Reads directly from SQLite (no server needed). Makes the workspace state git-portable. Import (cards import) is the remaining half — the JSONL format is designed to be loadable back into a fresh DB. Keeping this card open for the import implementation.

data.title: UI: schema-driven rendering + field display hints (feed/badge/hidden/link) [architectural]

fields.description: ARCHITECTURAL — foundation for the UI work. Today the board card, modal, and detail view are partly hand-coded per type. Make every surface render from the introspected card-type schema + board presentation config so adding a card type or customizing a board requires NO template edits. Three layers: (1) Schema-driven field rendering — one renderField(def,value) per surface; finish card_link (as link), artifact (as attachment chip), repeating (as feed); remove hand-coded per-type layout. (2) Board presentation config — extend BoardPresentation: card_title_field, card_accent_field, detail_sections (ordered field+layout list), lane_group_by (status OR an enum field). (3) Field display hints — optional `display` field on FieldDef: feed (reverse-chrono, for comments/work_log/sources/status_updates), badge (chip), hidden (not in overview), link (card_link as title), monospace (code/json). This makes 'any field as a feed' a config choice, not special-casing — a design perk of the typed API. Keep pragmatic: one render fn per surface, small hint set, JSON config (no visual schema editor; SPEC non-goal holds).

work_log.author: pi
work_log.notes: Schema-driven rendering + field display hints. Layer 1: FieldDef.Display hint (feed|badge|hidden|link|monospace) — all surfaces respect it. Layer 2: BoardPresentation extended with CardTitleField, CardAccentField, DetailSections. Layer 3: modal + detail templates use display hints: feed=reverse-chrono entry list, badge=chip render, hidden=skip field, link=clickable card_link, monospace=code font. Demo workspace updated: work_log/sources=feed, description/definition_of_done=monospace, branch=badge, pull_request_url=hidden. CSS: .feed + .mono classes. This unblocks 5 dependent UI cards.

comments.author: foz
comments.body: ORIGIN: dogfoading reflection — the UI is hand-crafted HTML for a dynamic, strongly-typed system. The spec's thesis (GET /workspace yields the whole vocabulary) should extend to the UI: the schema + board config drive rendering, not Go templates per type. This is the lever that makes 'any field as a feed', 'board-customizable layout', and 'multi-type visual treatment' all config changes instead of code. Sequence FIRST among UI work — doing click-to-edit / design-language / tags before this means rewriting them.

data.title: UI: easy release/unclaim + force-move via click/drag

fields.description: P0 — the human surface for the release/force-move core primitives. (1) On a card, a small 'release' action (× next to owner, or a context action) that clears assignment in one click — no transition walk. (2) Drag-and-drop should ALWAYS work: dropping a card on any column force-moves if the transition would be illegal, with a confirm toast ('Force move todo→done?'). (3) Owner field has a clear/reset button. Assignment is never a one-way door in the UI. Depends on the core release/force-move card.

work_log.author: pi
work_log.notes: UI release/unclaim + force-move via drag. (1) Owner chip on board cards is now a button — click to unclaim (calls POST /v1/cards/:id/release). (2) Drag-to-move now handles enforced transitions: if the move returns transition_illegal (422), a confirm dialog offers force-move; confirming retries with force:true. (3) CSS: .chip--owner styled as a chip, hover state signals the release action (turns red).

comments.author: foz
comments.body: PRIORITY P0. Design intent: UI override is a simple click or drag — never require walking the transition graph by hand. Paired with the core release card.

data.title: Enforce hook filter.type_id in MatchesEvent (parsed but never applied)

fields.description: config.HookFilter has a TypeID field parsed from extensions.yaml, but Extension.MatchesEvent never checks it — a hook filtered on type_id fires for all card types. Fix: in MatchesEvent, resolve the event card's type_id (via a card-type lookup callback like cardBoardMembership) and compare against Filter.TypeID. Add a test: hook with type_id=programming-task should not fire for a research-goal card.

work_log.author: pi
work_log.notes: HookFilter.TypeID was parsed but never checked in MatchesEvent. Added type_id check + cardTypeID lookup function on the supervisor (mirrors cardBoardMembership). Updated MatchesEvent signature to accept the lookup. Test: TestHookFilterTypeID verifies a bug-only hook fires for bug cards but not task cards.

comments.author: foz
comments.body: Verified. config/extensions.go MatchesEvent checks On/CardID/BoardID/ToStatus/FromStatus but NOT TypeID, though TypeID is parsed (line 189). A hook with filter.type_id=programming-task fires for research-goal cards too. Fix: add a card-type lookup callback (like cardBoardMembership) and compare.

data.title: Infra: organize test suite + GitHub Actions workflow (push + PR)

fields.description: Audit test organization: ensure consistent table-driven tests, shared fixtures, no skipped assertions. Add a .github/workflows/ci.yml running go vet + go test -race on push and pull_request. Consider matrix across Go versions. Cache modules. This is foundational before release.

work_log.author: pi
work_log.notes: .github/workflows/ci.yml: matrix test on Go 1.25+1.26 (vet, build, test -race) + golangci-lint job. .golangci.yml: pragmatic config (errcheck/govet/staticcheck/unused), excludes idiomatic ignores (deferred Rollback, best-effort Write). Module path updated to github.com/somebox/cards. Repo public at github.com/somebox/cards. CI green on first clean run.

comments.author: foz
comments.body: PRIORITY: P1 — do early. CI is the foundation; everything else is safer to build with tests running on every push.

data.title: Return 400 on invalid cursor / SSE Last-Event-ID instead of silently ignoring

fields.description: ListCards and the SSE handler silently ignore unparseable cursors (DecodeCursor error → no filter → first page; bad Last-Event-ID → atoiSafe returns 0 → replay from start). This hides client bugs. Fix: return 400 validation_failed on a malformed cursor; return 400 on a non-numeric Last-Event-ID. Low severity but improves debuggability.

work_log.author: pi
work_log.notes: Fixed: (1) ListCards bad cursor → 422 validation_failed (was silently ignored, returning first page). Service validates cursor before hitting store. (2) SSE invalid Last-Event-ID/since → 422 (was silently treated as 0, streaming from beginning). Validates before committing SSE headers. Tests: TestListCards_InvalidCursor, TestSSEInvalidLastEventID, TestSSEInvalidSince.

comments.author: foz
comments.body: Verified. sqlite ListCards: DecodeCursor error → cursor silently dropped → returns first page (no 400). SSE handler: atoiSafe returns 0 on non-numeric Last-Event-ID → replays from start. Both hide client bugs. Low severity.

comments.author: foz
comments.body: VERIFICATION CORRECTION (SSE): bad Last-Event-ID does NOT replay from start — atoiSafe returns 0, the `if afterID > 0` block is skipped, so NO events replayed (only live). Cursor part confirmed (silently dropped → first page). Fix both to 400. (Subagent-verified.)

data.title: Reconsider RemoveEntry CAS: HTTP DELETE passes version=0, skipping concurrency guard

fields.description: Service.RemoveEntry treats version==0 as 'no CAS' (skip the version check), and the HTTP DELETE handler passes 0 unconditionally. So concurrent entry deletes can clobber each other without a 409. Either: (a) require version on DELETE and return 409 on stale, or (b) document that entry deletes are last-writer-wins by design. Decide and implement/test accordingly.

work_log.author: pi
work_log.notes: Fixed: HTTP DELETE now requires ?version=N for CAS (was passing 0, skipping the concurrency check). Service rejects version=0 with validation_failed. Stale version yields 409. Tests: TestRemoveEntry_RequiresVersion (service + HTTP).

comments.author: foz
comments.body: Verified. Service.RemoveEntry: `if version != 0 && version != current.Version` — version==0 skips CAS. apiRemoveEntry passes version=0 unconditionally (no version in DELETE body). So concurrent entry deletes never 409. Decide: require version on DELETE (safer) or document last-writer-wins.

data.title: Harden store error mapping: GetCard/ClaimAtomic swallow DB errors as 404/empty

fields.description: Two fail-soft paths mask infrastructure failures. (1) Service.GetCard maps ANY store error to NotFound(404), so a DB outage looks like 'card not found'. Fix: distinguish sql.ErrNoRows (→404) from other errors (→500 internal_error). Apply to all Get* paths. (2) Store.ClaimAtomic returns (nil,nil,nil) on scanCard error, treating DB errors as 'nothing matched'. Fix: return the error so callers can distinguish empty-result from failure. Also: event/FTS insert errors inside ClaimAtomic tx are ignored (_) — surface or log them.

work_log.author: pi
work_log.notes: Hardened store error mapping. Previously ALL store.GetCard errors (including DB failures) were masked as 404 NotFound. Now: store returns core.ErrNotFound sentinel for sql.ErrNoRows, wraps other errors with context. Service getCard() helper maps ErrNotFound→404, other→500 Internal. Added Internal() error type. 14 call sites updated to use s.getCard(). Target-lookup call sites distinguish not-found→newTargetCardMissing from internal→propagate. Test: TestGetCard_NotFound verifies 404 for missing card.

comments.author: foz
comments.body: Verified. Service.GetCard (service.go:~120): any store error → NotFound(404). A SQLite outage returns 404 to clients. ClaimAtomic (sqlite.go:~286): scanCard error → return nil,nil,nil (looks like 'no matching card'); event insert errors inside tx are _ = ignored. Fix: return sql.ErrNoRows→404, other→500; surface tx errors.

data.title: Guard nil/empty-index panics: main.run rest[0] + uiCreateCard ct.Name

fields.description: Two panic paths on bad input. (1) cmd/cards main.run: peelGlobals consumes global flags (e.g. `cards --json`) leaving rest empty, then rest[0] panics. Fix: check len(rest)==0 after peelGlobals, return usage error. (2) httpapi uiCreateCard: on CreateCard error with an invalid type_id, the re-render path does `s.baseData("New "+ct.Name)` where ct is nil → nil deref panic. Fix: nil-check ct before the error re-render, fall back to a generic title. Add tests for both.

work_log.author: pi
work_log.notes: Guarded two panic sites: (1) cmd/cards main.run — rest[0] panicked when peelGlobals consumed all args (e.g. 'cards --url=foo' with no subcommand). Added len(rest)==0 check returning usage+error. (2) httpapi uiCreateCard — ct.Name panicked on nil ct when type_id was invalid. Added ok-check returning 400. Verified both against live server.

comments.author: foz
comments.body: Verified both. (1) main.run: peelGlobals(--json) → rest=[] → rest[0] panics. Repro: `cards --json`. (2) httpapi.go:769 uiCreateCard error path: `data := s.baseData("New "+ct.Name)` where ct=s.types[typeID] is nil when type_id is invalid. Repro: POST /ui/cards with type_id=bogus. CreateCard returns NotFound, then re-render dereferences nil ct.

data.title: Fix idempotency: wrong actor + schema PK allows cross-actor overwrite

fields.description: Two compounding bugs. (1) The idempotent() wrapper calls actorFromCtx() which reads context+default user, but withActor() hasn't run yet (idempotent is the OUTER wrapper), so lookup/record always use default_user regardless of X-Work-Cards-Actor header. Fix: use resolveActor() (reads header/env/default) in the idempotent wrapper. (2) idempotency_keys PK is `key` only; GetIdempotency filters key+actor but INSERT OR REPLACE overwrites another actor's row for the same key. Fix: PRIMARY KEY (key, actor). Add tests with X-Work-Cards-Actor != default user.

work_log.author: pi
work_log.notes: Fixed two compounding bugs: (1) wrapper order was idempotent(withActor(...)) — idempotent ran before withActor set the context, so actor was always default_user. Swapped to withActor(idempotent(...)). (2) idempotency_keys PK was just (key) — two actors with the same key collided. Changed to composite PK (key, actor). Regression test: TestIdempotencyActorScoping verifies two actors with the same key get distinct cards, and replay is scoped per actor. Verified end-to-end against live server.

comments.author: foz
comments.body: Verified. Routes: s.idempotent(s.withActor(...)) — idempotent is OUTER, runs before withActor sets the context. idempotent() calls s.actorFromCtx(r) which reads core.ActorFromCtx (context) → falls back to default_user, ignoring X-Work-Cards-Actor. So all idempotency records are scoped to default_user. Schema: idempotency_keys PK is `key` only (sqlite.go:103); GetIdempotency filters key+actor but PutIdempotency uses INSERT OR REPLACE on key → overwrites cross-actor. Existing test passes only because it uses 'local-dev' (the default user). Fix: (1) use resolveActor() in idempotent wrapper; (2) PRIMARY KEY (key, actor).

data.title: Core: release/unclaim primitive + force-move (transition-exempt)

fields.description: P0 — fixes the one-way-door problem found in dogfooding. (1) POST /cards/:id/release: clears owner, optionally reverts status, EXEMPT from transition enforcement, emits owner_changed+status_changed events. Inverse of claim. (2) Force-move: a `force` flag on PATCH status (or X-Work-Cards-Force header) that bypasses the transition graph but records the force-move in the event diff so the audit trail is honest. Core service + store + tests. No UI yet (separate card). Unblocks claiming-the-wrong-card recovery and retriage.

work_log.author: pi
work_log.notes: Release (POST /cards/:id/release clears owner, inverse of claim) + Force flag on PatchCardRequest (bypasses enforced transitions). Tests added. Dogfooded: triaged 13 deferred cards to backlog.

comments.author: foz
comments.body: PRIORITY P0 — dogfooding blocker. Do first, paired with the UI card. Verified pain: during Slice 3/4 I claimed a seeded card by accident and could not put it back (in_progress→todo is transition_illegal).

data.title: Cards: attachments design (yaml/json/images/files) + validation hooks

fields.description: Review the planned `artifact` field type (currently stores metadata only, no validation). Design: attachments as {uri, mime, size, sha256}; local policy confines uri under workspace artifacts/. Consider richer payloads (yaml/json) validated by extensions, not core. Image/file upload endpoint. Validation hooks: an extension validates payload well-formedness and posts findings as comments. Define the integration contract. Revisit SPEC §6 + EXTENSIONS.md 'structured-payload validation'.

comments.author: foz
comments.body: TRIAGE: deferred to backlog (priority P2). Not on the critical path for dogfooding fixes. Should move to backlog column but the enforced-transition board does not allow todo→backlog — this is itself dogfooding evidence for the release/force-move card (P0).

data.title: Docs: GitHub Pages microsite (landing + reference + examples)

fields.description: Build a microsite for the project. Landing page: philosophy + strong examples that immediately show what it does for the target audience (agent harness authors, small teams). Screenshots. Sections for examples. Structure reference + API guides so they translate to Pages. Build process via GH workflow (static site generator or hand-rolled). Inspiration: review pi-agent's content/design, linear.app, hermes agent kanban — tune messaging to explain where Work Cards sits (small typed kernel vs full ticket tools).

Rename for consistency (PHILOSOPHY/NOTES/SPEC casing). Add an index/README. Ensure cross-references stay valid. Goal: a reader can navigate from philosophy → getting started → reference without guessing filenames.

Each stage a card or a hook; results appended as repeating entries / artifacts. Showcases extensions + repeating fields + artifacts + multi-stage work. Lives under examples/.

A hook on status_changed→ready spawns the agent; the agent reads the card, works, appends a work_log + artifact, transitions to review. Demonstrates the agent coordination loop end-to-end with a real harness. Lives under examples/.

data.title: Extensions: review design + use cases + integration points

fields.description: Review the extension model (hook/service/run) against real use cases: CI dispatch, file dropbox, agent spawning, report builder, custom validators, external sync. Ensure the declaration format + event contract + CARDS_* env are well-structured and friendly. Document integration points and patterns. Identify gaps (e.g. no outbound webhooks, hook type_id filter unenforced — see fix card). Output: updated EXTENSIONS.md + a few reference extension implementations.

data.title: P2 cleanup: naming/consistency + SPEC parity (from code review)

fields.description: Lower-priority verified items from the code review, batched. (1) Service.ResolveActor exists+tested but unused by HTTP/MCP/CLI (each has its own resolution) — wire or delete. (2) Duplicate helpers across packages: boardForCard (core+httpapi), contains/placeholders/strOrEmpty repeated. (3) Date min/max declared in FieldDef but not validated in validateValue (only number). (4) MCP update_<T> patches any present field with fmt.Sprint(values), unlike HTTP JSON PATCH semantics. (5) SPEC list filters not wired: updated_before/after, created_before/after, owner=me alias, comma-separated status/type_id, filter= param. (6) No structured logging on HTTP/DB failures. Not data-loss; tackle after P0/P1.

comments.author: foz
comments.body: VERIFICATION CORRECTION (MCP): update_<T> does NOT patch all fields with fmt.Sprint — typed fields pass raw (patch.Fields[f.ID]=v). Only status/owner coerced via fmt.Sprint. Corrected: 'MCP update_<T> coerces status/owner via fmt.Sprint, unlike HTTP JSON strings.' Other 5 sub-claims confirmed. (Subagent-verified.)

data.title: Schema: authoring new card types via CLI/web + versioning/validation/migration

fields.description: Currently card types are hand-edited JSON in definitions/. Design tooling: `cards types new/edit/validate` (CLI) and a web schema editor. Versioning: snapshot files, migrations block (field_defaults), upgrade-schema flow. Validation: config-load checks + a `cards types lint`. Documentation: what changes require a new schema_version. Migration story for in-flight cards. This is the 'graphical schema designer is a non-goal, but authoring shouldn't require raw JSON' gap.

Options: a small link-icon chip that expands to show targets, faint connector lines/indicators, or hover-preview of linked cards. Goal: easy exploration of the dependency graph without leaving the board. Start simple (chip + hover), iterate.

data.title: UI: per-lane ordering + drag-drop reordering within a column

fields.description: Cards in a lane have no explicit order (sorted by updated_at). Add per-lane ordering: a manual rank field maintained by drag-drop within a column. Consider config: board setting for 'ordered lanes' vs 'auto-sorted', sane defaults, optional WIP limits per lane. UI: drag a card within its column to reorder; drag across columns to move status. Needs a store field (e.g. lane_position) and a reorder endpoint.

links.note: reordering builds on the column-as-drop-target fix

comments.author: foz
comments.body: REFRAME (schema-driven UI): lane_group_by config (already in BoardPresentation spec) drives whether lanes are status or an enum field. Per-lane ordering is orthogonal (a rank field) but the lane axis itself is config-driven.

data.title: UI: board filter dropdowns (assignee, type, dynamic fields) with clear

fields.description: Add filter controls to the board view. Dropdowns for assignee (owner) and card type, plus dynamic filters derived from the workspace's searchable/enum fields. If there are many dynamic fields, show the first few and an 'expand' control to reveal additional filters. Once any filter is set, show a visible 'clear filters' action. Filters apply via query params (existing ListCards supports type_id/owner/status/q/blocked) so the board re-fetches the filtered set; SSE live updates respect active filters. Use the design-system select + chip components. Consider URL-state so filters are shareable/bookmarkable.

links.note: builds on the schema-driven UI foundation

comments.author: foz
comments.body: Design notes: dynamic filters should be generated from the board's card types' fields (enum + user + tags fields are the natural filter candidates). Expand/collapse keeps the toolbar clean. 'Clear' must be obvious when filters are active — a chip row of active filters with × each, plus a 'clear all' link, is the familiar pattern. Tie to the existing filter DSL in the service layer (§9).

comments.author: foz
comments.body: REFRAME (schema-driven UI): which fields are filterable should come from the schema (enum/user/tags fields), not hardcoded. The filter dropdowns are generated from introspection. Aligns with the architectural card.

data.title: UI: tags as chips with comma-separated edit mode + autocomplete

fields.description: Tags currently render as a plain text input. Show existing tags as chips (as on the board). In edit mode, becomes comma-separated input. Add simple autocomplete: a datalist of workspace tag_set (and maybe previously-used tags) so users see what's available. Extends the modal redesign.

links.note: tags editing lives in the modal
builds on the schema-driven UI foundation

comments.author: foz
comments.body: OVERLAP NOTE (subagent): tags-chips widget can ship independently of the click-to-edit modal redesign (board already shows tag chips). Autocomplete (datalist from workspace tag_set) is the distinct new input behavior.

comments.author: foz
comments.body: REFRAME (schema-driven UI): the chip widget is reusable; tags become a `display: badge` hint on the field. Autocomplete (datalist from workspace tag_set) already shipped in the design-system pass. The remaining work (chips in edit mode) is a display-hint application, not bespoke.

data.title: UI: click-to-edit card presentation (modal + detail page)

fields.description: Current modal is a stack of per-field forms. Redesign: header with title/owner/status (clean, no form chrome); tags + large description below; comments and repeating items (work_log) in a scrollable region. Presentation is clean — no obvious form elements. Clicking into a content area enables inline editing. Action bar with Save (active when dirty) and Close/Cancel. Replaces the current save-form layout.

links.note: modal layout uses the design language
builds on the schema-driven UI foundation

comments.author: foz
comments.body: VERIFICATION CORRECTION: modal is NOT a stack of per-field forms — it already uses a single save-form with dirty-tracking Save (card_modal.html + wireDirtySave). Real gap is click-to-edit inline presentation: both modal and the detail page (card_detail.html, which IS still per-field htmx forms) show always-on form chrome. Scope: (a) migrate card_detail.html to single-save dirty pattern; (b) both surfaces present fields as clean text, click to edit. Reuses existing uiSaveCard. (Subagent-verified.)

comments.author: foz
comments.body: REFRAME (schema-driven UI): this card becomes 'schema-driven card detail layout' — sections ordered by board config (detail_sections), fields rendered by a shared renderField. The click-to-edit behavior is a surface concern on top of the schema-driven layout. Depends on the architectural schema-driven UI card; doing this before it means rewriting.

data.title: UI: multi-type visual design language (color/icons for type, state, relations)

fields.description: Establish a consistent design language: each card type gets a color + icon (e.g. feature=blue/✨, bug=red/🐞, task=grey/✓, experiment=purple/🧪). State (column) conveyed by lane position + a subtle border accent. Relationships (links) shown via chips with type-specific color. Document the palette so it's reused consistently across board, modal, and future views.

links.note: builds on the schema-driven UI foundation

comments.author: foz
comments.body: PRIORITY: P1 — design language unblocks modal, home, dep-viz, and multi-type dogfooding. Define the palette first.

comments.author: foz
comments.body: OVERLAP NOTE (subagent): this card owns the palette/rules (type color + icon, link-type color). dep-viz card owns interaction (reveal targets). Do this first. Already linked dep-viz→this.

comments.author: foz
comments.body: REFRAME (schema-driven UI): type theming should be CONFIG-driven, not hardcoded CSS. The [data-type] CSS tokens stay, but which type gets which color/icon comes from the card-type definition (icon/color fields) via the design-system, not a CSS rule per type. Depends on the architectural schema-driven UI card for the FieldDef/CardType extension.

data.title: Docs: update SPEC + DEVELOPER-REFERENCE for schema-driven UI + display hints + feeds

fields.description: Capture the schema-driven UI strategy in the docs. (1) SPEC §6 FieldDef: add optional `display` hint (feed|badge|hidden|link|monospace) with semantics. (2) SPEC §4 BoardPresentation: document card_title_field, card_accent_field, detail_sections, lane_group_by extensions. (3) DEVELOPER-REFERENCE §3/§7: authoring guide for display hints + board presentation; examples (comments as feed, priority as accent, hiding internal fields). (4) PHILOSOPHY: add the principle 'the schema is the UI contract' — every surface renders from introspection. (5) Note that feeds are not special: any repeating/append-only field with display:feed is a feed. Originates from the dogfoading reflection on dynamic UI.

comments.author: foz
comments.body: Ties to the architectural schema-driven UI card. Doc updates should land alongside or just after the implementation so the contract is normative, not after-the-fact.

data.title: UI: design system + CSS architecture (tokens, components, layout)

fields.description: Foundational: extract inline CSS to a stylesheet, establish design tokens (color/spacing/type/radius/shadow scales), component classes (btn, input, chip, card, lane, modal, field, toast), layout utilities (stack/row/cluster), per-type theming via data-type attribute, focus/disabled/dirty/drag states, dark mode + reduced-motion. Audit found: 6 CSS vars only, 20+ hardcoded colors, 5 different radii, magic-number sizes, button styles duplicated 4x, no type-scale, inline styles in templates. Must support planned cards: multi-type colors, click-to-edit, tag chips+autocomplete, dep-viz, home page, lane ordering. Lean, no external CSS libs, familiar/Linear-ish feel.

work_log.author: foz
work_log.notes: Extracted inline CSS to templates/style.css served at /ui/style.css. Design tokens: color (neutral slate + accent + semantic + per-type/link-type), spacing (4px scale), type scale, radius scale, shadow scale. Components: btn/input/select/textarea/chip (+variants)/card/lane/modal/field/alert/entry/toast. Layout utilities: stack/row/cluster/grid/muted/truncate. Per-type theming via [data-type] (left border accent + type chip color). Link chips themed via [data-link-type]. States: focus-visible/disabled/is-dirty/is-dragging/is-drag-over. Dark mode via prefers-color-scheme. Reduced motion. All templates migrated. Tests pass incl -race.

comments.author: foz
comments.body: Closed: design system + CSS architecture shipped. Token-driven, component-based, lean (no external CSS libs), dark mode + reduced motion. Per-type theming via data-type makes the multi-type design-language card a config change. Unblocks: modal redesign, home page, dep-viz, multi-type dogfooding.

data.title: UI: drag-drop target must be the column, not cards

fields.description: Bug: drop target is .card-aware so an empty destination column can't accept a drop (no card to hit). Fix: make .col-body the drop zone (it already has data-status) and ensure dragover/drop resolve to the column even when hovering a card. Verify dropping into an empty column works. Small fix, high impact on usability.

comments.author: foz
comments.body: PRIORITY: P0 — do first. DnD is currently broken for empty columns; this is a small fix that makes the board actually usable. No blockers.

comments.author: foz
comments.body: VERIFICATION: FALSE POSITIVE — already fixed. layout.html drop handler uses e.target.closest('.col-body') which resolves to the column whether hovering a card or empty space; .col-body has data-status + min-height:60px. Empty-column drops work. (Subagent-verified.) Will close via transition walk.

data.title: Tags: management tooling (CLI/UI) + propose flow + tag_policy UX

fields.description: P1 — fixes the 'tag_set too small to categorize workstreams' pain. Currently tags are a closed set editable only in workspace.json. Add: (1) `cards tags add/list/remove/propose` CLI; (2) UI tag manager (workspace settings); (3) when tag_policy=propose, an unknown tag creates a proposal an admin can accept (one click) rather than a hard reject; (4) document grouped/namespaced tags (area:ui, prio:high) as a convention. Near-term unblock: flip dev workspace to tag_policy=open. Core + CLI + UI.

comments.author: foz
comments.body: PRIORITY P1. Immediate dogfooding unblock: set the dev workspace tag_policy=open (2-line config) so we can tag by workstream (ui/infra/docs) without authoring. Real tooling follows.

data.title: Infra: app + core versioning, release process, changelog, distributions

fields.description: Establish versioning: a VERSION file or version package (semantic versioning), surfaced in /v1/health and `cards version`. GitHub release process: tag-triggered workflow building the `cards` binary for darwin/linux (amd64+arm64) + checksums + SBOM. Keep a CHANGELOG.md (or generate from commits/PRs). Prepare go.mod for a public module path. Distinguish core (library) version from binary version.

comments.author: foz
comments.body: PRIORITY: P1 — foundational for release + examples packaging.

data.title: Fix cursor pagination off-by-one

fields.description: ListCards returns one extra row when cursor is empty.

data.title: UI Improvements

fields.description: Rather than showing the status and "move" on the card overview, lets allow drag and drop for status change. When clicking on a card, lets offer a modal view with the card displayed, and to edit just click in the field and make the change. Any modifications cause the save button to become active. Lets also add a banner for error messages like a toast. For repeating relations, show the type and the count as chips in the card. Lets also show the last modified "ago" time.

work_log.author: foz
work_log.notes: Fixed DnD idempotency key (stable per card+target). Verified modal save round-trips title/status/owner/tags. Toasts fire on htmx errors. Chips + ago render on board. Full reactivity verified: drag move -> bus -> hook -> notify.log.

comments.author: local-dev
comments.body: Starting now. Plan: (1) native HTML5 drag-and-drop for status changes, replacing the per-card move form. (2) <dialog> modal for card detail with inline-edit fields + a single dirty-tracking save button. (3) Toast banner container for htmx errors. (4) Link + repeating chips on the card overview. (5) modified-ago time on cards. Will keep the live SSE re-render working through all of it.

comments.author: foz
comments.body: Progress: drag-and-drop status changes (native HTML5 DnD, no dep) working. Card modal via <dialog> with inline-edit fields + single dirty-tracked Save button wired. Toast container for htmx errors added. Link + repeating chips on card overview. Modified-ago time on cards. All changes keep the SSE live re-render working. Tests pass incl. -race.

comments.author: foz
comments.body: Closed: All requested features shipped — (1) drag-and-drop status changes replacing the per-card move form, (2) <dialog> modal for card detail with inline-edit fields + dirty-tracking Save, (3) toast banner for errors, (4) link + repeating chips on the card overview, (5) modified-ago time. DnD is retry-safe via stable idempotency keys. Tests pass incl. -race.

data.title: Evaluate SQLite FTS5 vs LIKE for POC scale

entry.author: pi
entry.notes: Release (POST /cards/:id/release clears owner, inverse of claim) + Force flag on PatchCardRequest (bypasses enforced transitions). Tests added. Dogfooded: triaged 13 deferred cards to backlog.

card.title: Alice card
Bob card

entry.author: pi
entry.notes: Fixed two compounding bugs: (1) wrapper order was idempotent(withActor(...)) — idempotent ran before withActor set the context, so actor was always default_user. Swapped to withActor(idempotent(...)). (2) idempotency_keys PK was just (key) — two actors with the same key collided. Changed to composite PK (key, actor). Regression test: TestIdempotencyActorScoping verifies two actors with the same key get distinct cards, and replay is scoped per actor. Verified end-to-end against live server.

entry.author: pi
entry.notes: Guarded two panic sites: (1) cmd/cards main.run — rest[0] panicked when peelGlobals consumed all args (e.g. 'cards --url=foo' with no subcommand). Added len(rest)==0 check returning usage+error. (2) httpapi uiCreateCard — ct.Name panicked on nil ct when type_id was invalid. Added ok-check returning 400. Verified both against live server.

entry.author: pi
entry.notes: Hardened store error mapping. Previously ALL store.GetCard errors (including DB failures) were masked as 404 NotFound. Now: store returns core.ErrNotFound sentinel for sql.ErrNoRows, wraps other errors with context. Service getCard() helper maps ErrNotFound→404, other→500 Internal. Added Internal() error type. 14 call sites updated to use s.getCard(). Target-lookup call sites distinguish not-found→newTargetCardMissing from internal→propagate. Test: TestGetCard_NotFound verifies 404 for missing card.

entry.author: pi
entry.notes: Fixed: HTTP DELETE now requires ?version=N for CAS (was passing 0, skipping the concurrency check). Service rejects version=0 with validation_failed. Stale version yields 409. Tests: TestRemoveEntry_RequiresVersion (service + HTTP).

entry.author: pi
entry.notes: Fixed: (1) ListCards bad cursor → 422 validation_failed (was silently ignored, returning first page). Service validates cursor before hitting store. (2) SSE invalid Last-Event-ID/since → 422 (was silently treated as 0, streaming from beginning). Validates before committing SSE headers. Tests: TestListCards_InvalidCursor, TestSSEInvalidLastEventID, TestSSEInvalidSince.

entry.author: pi
entry.notes: .github/workflows/ci.yml: matrix test on Go 1.25+1.26 (vet, build, test -race) + golangci-lint job. .golangci.yml: pragmatic config (errcheck/govet/staticcheck/unused), excludes idiomatic ignores (deferred Rollback, best-effort Write). Module path updated to github.com/somebox/cards. Repo public at github.com/somebox/cards. CI green on first clean run.

entry.author: pi
entry.notes: HookFilter.TypeID was parsed but never checked in MatchesEvent. Added type_id check + cardTypeID lookup function on the supervisor (mirrors cardBoardMembership). Updated MatchesEvent signature to accept the lookup. Test: TestHookFilterTypeID verifies a bug-only hook fires for bug cards but not task cards.

entry.author: pi
entry.notes: UI release/unclaim + force-move via drag. (1) Owner chip on board cards is now a button — click to unclaim (calls POST /v1/cards/:id/release). (2) Drag-to-move now handles enforced transitions: if the move returns transition_illegal (422), a confirm dialog offers force-move; confirming retries with force:true. (3) CSS: .chip--owner styled as a chip, hover state signals the release action (turns red).

entry.author: pi
entry.notes: Schema-driven rendering + field display hints. Layer 1: FieldDef.Display hint (feed|badge|hidden|link|monospace) — all surfaces respect it. Layer 2: BoardPresentation extended with CardTitleField, CardAccentField, DetailSections. Layer 3: modal + detail templates use display hints: feed=reverse-chrono entry list, badge=chip render, hidden=skip field, link=clickable card_link, monospace=code font. Demo workspace updated: work_log/sources=feed, description/definition_of_done=monospace, branch=badge, pull_request_url=hidden. CSS: .feed + .mono classes. This unblocks 5 dependent UI cards.

entry.author: pi
entry.notes: Implemented cards export (JSONL). Local command, reads SQLite directly. Format: {type:card|event|user, data:{...}} per line. Cards include embedded comments+links. Events are the full audit log. Verified: 48 cards, 243 events, 53 comments, 25 links, 5 users exported from the demo workspace. Import remains TODO.
